lede: A look at July 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Suzan Lamens and Fabrício de Souza, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Keir Starmer
events.desc: Keir Starmer is appointed Prime Minister by Charles III, becoming the first Labour prime minister since Gordon Brown in 2010 and the first one to win a general election since Tony Blair at the 2005 general election

events.title: The Shard
events.desc: The Shard in London is inaugurated as the tallest building in Europe, with a height of 310 metres (1,020 ft).

events.title: July 2009 Ürümqi riots
events.desc: A series of violent riots break out in Ürümqi, the capital city of the Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region in China.

events.title: Cape Verde
events.desc: Cape Verde gains its independence from Portugal.

events.title: Juvénal Habyarimana
events.desc: Juvénal Habyarimana seizes power over Rwanda in a coup d'état.

events.title: Elvis Presley
events.desc: Elvis Presley records his first single, "That's All Right", at Sun Records in Memphis, Tennessee.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: Task Force Smith: American and North Korean forces first clash, in the Battle of Osan.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Foreign relations of Vichy France are severed with the United Kingdom.

events.title: Midway Atoll
events.desc: The United States discovers and claims Midway Atoll.

events.title: Thomas Cook
events.desc: Thomas Cook organises the first package excursion, from Leicester to Loughborough.

deaths.text: Hugh Shearer, Jamaican journalist and politician, 3rd Prime Minister of Jamaica
Leo McCarey, American director, producer, and screenwriter
Albrecht Kossel, German physician and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Anthony Maria Zaccaria, priest (died 1539)
Christian feast day: Cyril and Methodius (a public holiday in Czech Republic and Slovakia)
Christian feast day: Wexford Martyrs (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Zoe of Rome (Roman Catholic Church)
Fifth of July (New York), historic celebration of the abolition of slavery in New York in 1827.
Independence Day (Algeria), celebrating the independence of Algeria from France in 1962.
